name: Applicative Functor
def: A functor that can apply a wrapped function to a wrapped value, combining independent effects.
problem: Combine multiple independent effectful values without sequencing one on another (unlike monads).
mechanism: Provides pure (lift a value) and ap : F<A→B> → F<A> → F<B>; effects are combined in parallel/independent fashion, not chained on prior results.
limits: Cannot depend a later effect on an earlier value (that needs a monad)
failures: Reaching for applicative where sequencing is required
whenNot: Effects that must be sequenced (use a monad)
myths: "Applicative = monad" — every monad is applicative, not vice versa; applicatives keep effects independent
